div.head: The Ambassador in China (Stuart) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 529. [To Consul General Clubb at Changchun.] Yenan Liaison Group withdrawn to Nanking today. Prior to [its] withdrawal, General Yeh Chien-yingRecently returned to Yenan from Peiping where he had been Chinese Communist Party Commissioner at Executive Headquarters. stated that Rigg and Collins were now en route Harbin and that General Chu Teh had ordered General Lin Piao to release them upon arrival Harbin.

Yenan and Harbin radio stations will broadcast for the next 5 days the date of their departure from Harbin, route to be followed, ETAEstimated time of arrival. at point of contact between Communists and National Government forces. Separate clear operational message has been sent instructing USISUnited States Information Service. to monitor Harbin and that you forward to Embassy any information on release Rigg and Collins obtained from Harbin broadcasts.

It will be your responsibility to pass necessary information to [appropriate Government]Bracketed insertion on basis of copy of telegram in Embassy files. Military Headquarters to permit safe reception Rigg and Collins at point of contact. It is desired that, if possible, you or CowenLt. Col. Edward T. Cowen, Assistant Military Attaché in China. be present at point of transfer to receive Rigg and Collins. Close liaison should be maintained with Northeast China Command and they requested take appropriate action to assure protection and prompt transportation Rigg and Collins to Changchun.
